rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HUMPHRIES VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against James A.Nelson, Co., Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 23, 2017 in Department 503, Line 3. Specially appearing Century Indemnity Company's motion for an order setting aside default and default judgment entered against James A. Nelson, Co., Inc. is granted. The December 7, 2012 entry of default against James A. Nelson Co., Inc. and the June 23, 2016 default judgment entered against James A. Nelson Co., Inc. are set aside. Plaintiff does not oppose the motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY CHARLIFUE et al VS. ALLIED PACKING & SUPPLY, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Appoint Successor-In-Interest To Deceased Plaintiff And To File First Amended Complaint/Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 23, 2017 in Department 503, Line 4. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to appoint Andrea Gerlach successor-in-interest to deceased plaintiff and to file first amended complaint is granted. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served as of the date of granting this motion on all defendants who have previously appeared. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: PETER MURCHISON VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 23, 2017 in Department 503, Line 5. Defendant Auto Row Parts Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of conclusively negating an element of plaintiff's cause of action pursuant to Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, which is the sole way defendant has attempted to sustain its burden. The evidence submitted by defendant - the declarations of Jerrold Cresta, Robert Giannini, and Nevin Brownfield, the Secretary of State detail, and the various correspondence with insurance companies - is insufficient to demonstrate that defendant does not have undistributed assets, including any insurance assets held by the corporation that may be available to satisfy claims. (Corp. Code § 2011(a)(1)(A).) Plaintiff's objections to the declarations of Jerrold Cresta and Robert Giannini are overruled. The Court did not consider defendant's reply evidence. (Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243; San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD SAMPSON JR. VS. CBS CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION, FKA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 23, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is continued to March 9, 2017 pursuant to Local Rule 2.7(B). Defendant failed to supply the Court with a courtesy copy of plaintiff's complaint. Plaintiff failed to lodge a courtesy copy of its opposition with the Court. Both sides must lodge their respective courtesy copies with Department 503 by February 23, 2017 at 4:00p.m. = (503/CML)
